steps.title: Find the target voltage
steps.content:
The capacitor starts at $16$ volts. The target is $15\%$ of that initial amount:
$$
0.15(16)=2.4
$$
So the graph should be read at about $2.4$ volts.

steps.title: Use the decay model
steps.content:
From the graph, the voltage follows exponential decay with initial value $16$. A model is
$$
V(t)=16e^{-t/\tau}
$$
where $\tau$ is the time constant.

steps.title: Estimate the time
steps.content: Set the model equal to the target voltage: $$ 16e^{-t/\tau}=2.4 $$ Then $$ e^{-t/\tau}=0.15 $$ so $$ t=-\tau\ln(0.15) $$ The graph shows $\tau$ is about $0.90$ seconds, so $$ t=-0.90\ln(0.15)=1.71 $$ The answer is C.
